summary: Listener is a first-time Dem voter in Rockville, MoCo, MD-8/D19/Council D7. They want bill-cited, name-cited, side-by-side reads on every Dem primary candidate on their actual ballot — not framings, not vibes. They have flagged five specific open questions repeatedly: (1) which candidates oppose county-level sanctuary policy, (2) Friedson's full fiscal record, (3) Wootton High School relocation and which candidates vote on the outcome, (4) Kramer's career-long voting record beyond the recent session, (5) a documented side-by-side across the candidates on their ballot.
themes: Bill-level receipts on each candidate, not narrative summaries — listener has asked daily.
Fiscal restraint and property tax math — rate-flat vs bill-flat distinction has not been made until this week.
Sanctuary policy specifics on county-level co-sponsorship: Trust Act (Bill 32-25), County Values Act (Bill 3-26).
Wootton High School: structural and political map of who actually has a vote on the outcome.
Career-long records, not just recent session — Kramer's House years (2007-2015) and Senate years (2015-now).
open_questions: Who among the Democratic primary candidates actually opposes sanctuary policy on illegal immigration? (Answered for the top 3 MoCo Exec candidates today: none of Friedson/Glass/Jawando — all are County Values Act co-sponsors.)

Is Andrew Friedson the candidate who stands for fiscal restriction and spending discipline? (Cleanest answer to date: yes on tax rate votes, with 4 documented no votes 2020/2023/2025/2026 on Elrich rate increases; complication is FY27 gap.)
Why does MCPS want to close and move Wootton High School, and what happens next?
What is Kramer's full historical voting record, including old news and public records beyond recent sessions?
What are the documented policy differences between each candidate on my primary ballot?
underserved_topics: Side-by-side ballot-wide comparison sheet on all Dem primary candidates
Tier-3 county races: State's Attorney, Sheriff, Clerk, Register of Wills
Tier-4 judicial races: 5 Sixth Judicial Circuit judges
MD-8 challenger reads: J.D. Kumar, Boris Kabel Velasquez, Stephen Alan Leon
17-filer At-Large Council field — listener votes for up to 4, only Elrich and Silvestre named so far
avoid_list_for_next_episode: The Charter Limit override mechanism on Friedson — used as the centerpiece of 2026-05-17 ep1 and the Friedson deep dive; do not reframe it for at least 3 episodes
Kumar Barve's 30-year House service framing — used in 2026-05-17 ep1 to introduce the PSC gas line pause; do not reuse as biographical intro
'rate flat does not mean bill flat' explainer on property tax mechanics — used in 2026-05-17 ep2; do not re-explain the assessment-vs-rate distinction
CASA in Action 143-endorsement story / Ferguson endorsement framing — used in 2026-05-17 ep2
Kramer 'reliable Democratic caucus discipline / not a defector' framing — used in 2026-05-17 ep3
Wes Moore Community Trust Act on-the-desk framing — used in 2026-05-17 ep3
Friedson '4 no votes on Elrich rate increases (2020/2023/2025/2026)' as fiscal-restraint receipt — used in deepdive_friedson, ep1, and ep2 same day
Friedson More Housing N.O.W. ZTA 25-02 8-3 vote framing — used in deepdive_friedson; do not reframe for 3 days
April 28 police-in-schools letter / April 30 Blake High shooting timing — used in deepdive_friedson; do not reuse
Lierman 'Maryland's hospital rate-setting waiver up for renegotiation in 2027' framing — used in 2026-05-17 ep3
